profile.title: AI Researcher & Developer
profile.address: Block E, Road: 9, Section: 1, Mirpur, Dhaka-1216
bioParagraphs: I am a Computer Science and Engineering undergraduate at Daffodil International University with a strong interest in Artificial Intelligence and healthcare research, focusing on medical image analysis, federated learning, and explainable AI.

As a Research Associate & Coordinator at the Health Informatics Research Lab (HIRL), I lead and contribute to AI-driven healthcare projects while mentoring junior researchers. I also gained industry experience as a Generative AI Intern at Metademy LLC, USA, working with large language models and AI agents.

I am passionate about developing intelligent, privacy-preserving, and clinically interpretable AI systems for real-world impact. I strongly believe in the importance of outreach in academia and actively mentor junior researchers, organize research workshops, and lead collaborative research programs.

education.description: Research Interests: Artificial Intelligence, Health Informatics, Machine Learning, Federated Learning, Explainable AI

papers.title: Beat-by-Beat Lead-Aware ECG Segmentation and Multi-Class Cardiac Disease Classification
papers.abstractHtml: This study proposes a beat-by-beat lead-aware ECG segmentation approach coupled with multi-class cardiac disease classification. Our method addresses the challenge of accurate ECG signal analysis by incorporating lead-specific information into the segmentation process, enabling more precise identification of cardiac waveforms and improving automated cardiac diagnostics through fine-grained signal analysis.

papers.title: Clinically Explainable Visual Question Answering (X-VQA) for Gastrointestinal Endoscopy
papers.abstractHtml: This work presents X-VQA, a clinically explainable Visual Question Answering framework designed specifically for gastrointestinal endoscopy. The system integrates visual and textual reasoning to provide interpretable diagnostic support, addressing the critical need for transparency in AI-assisted medical diagnosis.

papers.title: Federated Knowledge Distillation for Ear Disease Classification
papers.abstractHtml: This ongoing research explores federated knowledge distillation techniques for privacy-preserving ear disease classification across distributed medical datasets. By combining federated learning with knowledge distillation, we aim to train efficient models while maintaining patient data privacy.

papers.title: Privacy-Preserving Federated Vision Transformer with Grad-CAM Explainability for Multi-Label Chest X-Ray Classification
papers.abstractHtml: This ongoing work proposes a privacy-preserving federated Vision Transformer framework with Grad-CAM-based explainability for multi-label chest X-ray classification. The research addresses both data privacy concerns and clinical interpretability requirements.
